| 029 | 2026-05-20 tilemap perf /review-impl + finding O-1 reframe | The 2026-05-20 per-stage continent measurement (after #016 + #018 cleared) showed `place_zones` at **0.110 s** but the **modificator pipeline at 687.139 s** ‚Äî 99.98 % of the wall time. | 018 | 2026-05-16 tilemap Phase 1 human-in-loop review HIGH-1 | `penrose::assign_zone_tiles` is O(tiles √ó vertices) ‚Äî `nearest_vertex` linearly scans the full Penrose vertex field for every grid tile. At Continent scale (256¬≤ = 65 536 tiles √ó ~500-1200 vertices) that is ~10‚Å∑-10‚Å∏ `distance_sq` calls, single-threaded, vs the TMP_002 ¬ß7 budget. Structurally the same defect as #016 (fractalize) but larger ‚Äî the tile loop runs over the whole grid, not one zone ‚Äî and the capped AMAW Adversary caught only #016. Invisible at the 24¬≤-64¬≤ test fixtures. Fix: bucket vertices into a coarse uniform grid and test only neighbour buckets, or a multi-source BFS distance transform ‚Äî output stays identical. **2026-05-18 continent measurement (finding O-1) confirms the pain: a 256¬≤ `place_tilemap` takes ~8‚Äì11 min release-build ‚Äî recommend promoting to active work.** | Perf ‚Äî **profiling now shows pain (2026-05-18)** | MED |
| 017 | 2026-05-16 tilemap Phase 1 Adversary r1 WARN-3 | `force_directed_converge` computes `ConvergenceResult.converged` + `.iteration_count`, but `place_zones` / `place_tilemap` drop them ‚Äî `TilemapView` has no carrier, so a cap-hit tilemap is indistinguishable from a cleanly-converged one. PLAN chunk 0 said "add `iteration_count`/`converged` carriers as needed"; nothing in Phase 1 consumes them. Surface via a `GenerationMetadata` field on `TilemapView` when DP progress-streaming (TMP_002 ¬ß9 TMP-PLACE-Q3) lands. | Phase 2+ (progress-event streaming) | LOW |
| 016 | 2026-05-16 tilemap Phase 1 Adversary r1 WARN-2 | `fractalize::scatter_and_connect` is O(candidates¬≤) ‚Äî `nearest_cleared_dist_sq` linearly scans the growing `cleared` mask for every candidate. At Continent scale (256¬≤) that is ~10‚Å∏ ops per zone vs the TMP_002 ¬ß7 <500 ms budget; invisible at the 24¬≤-64¬≤ test fixtures. Replace the linear nearest-scan with coarse spatial buckets or a multi-source BFS distance transform. **2026-05-18 continent measurement (finding O-1) confirms the pain: a 256¬≤ `place_tilemap` takes ~8‚Äì11 min release-build ‚Äî recommend promoting to active work.** | Perf ‚Äî **profiling now shows pain (2026-05-18)** | MED |
| 015 | 2026-05-16 tilemap Phase 1 Adversary r1 WARN-1 (severity raised by human-in-loop review) | The wall-clock-cap fallback (`force_directed` D5) is a cross-machine layout divergence: a slow machine can trip the wall-clock cap where a fast one converges, so the grid-seed fallback differs across machines for the same seed ‚Äî the one path that can break the TMP-A4 axiom. | 008 | 2026-05-15 amaw-precommit-no-side-effect Adversary r1 WARN-1 | `load_state()` calls `json.loads()` with no try/except ‚Äî a corrupt or empty `.workflow-state.json` (manual edit, disk corruption) makes every gate command, incl. `cmd_amaw_pre_commit`, die with a traceback that blocks the commit. (Risk much reduced by DEFERRED #003's atomic save_state ‚Äî crashes can no longer half-write the file ‚Äî but manual-edit / disk-corruption paths remain.) Fix: wrap `load_state`'s parse in try/except; on corruption, print a clear error and either reset or refuse cleanly. Affects all `load_state` callers, so it is its own task ‚Äî not in #004's scope. | L4 hardening | LOW |
| 007 | 2026-05-15 amaw-task-slug-validation Adversary r2 WARN-1 | `cmd_amaw_enable` normalizes the slug only on the `if args:` write path; a no-arg or already-enabled invocation can leave a raw `state["task"]` persisted in the state file. Mitigated: `cmd_complete` defensively re-normalizes so the OUTPUT tag list is always comma-safe ‚Äî only the state-file display value can be stale. Fix: normalize unconditionally at the top of `cmd_amaw_enable`, or normalize on every state read. | L4 hardening | LOW |
| 001 | 2026-05-15 amaw-l3-deepen /review-impl #5 | `task_slug` not validated ‚Äî comma in slug splits into multiple tags downstream (`",".join(tags)` then mcp-query.py splits back on comma). No injection risk (subprocess list form). Fix: normalize comma ‚Üí dash in `cmd_amaw_enable`, or argparse-validate slug pattern `^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]$`. | L4 hardening | LOW |
| 002 | 2026-05-15 amaw-l3-deepen /review-impl #6 | `"REJECTED" in evidence.upper()` substring match in `cmd_complete` bridge logic ‚Äî false positives on "NOT REJECTED" or "rejected the rejected pattern". Fix: word-boundary regex `\bREJECTED\b` or check evidence prefix `"REJECTED:"` / `"status: REJECTED"`. | L4 hardening | LOW |
| 004 | 2026-05-15 amaw-l3-deepen /review-impl #8 | `cmd_pre_commit` early-exits silent when no STATE_FILE, but `&&`-chained `cmd_amaw_pre_commit` then calls `load_state()` which CREATES `.workflow-state.json` from INITIAL_STATE. Side effect: every commit by an agent NOT in any tracked task creates a stale state file, surfacing confusing `[ ]` markers on next `status`. Fix: in `cmd_amaw_pre_commit`, also early-exit if STATE_FILE absent before `load_state()`. | L4 hardening | LOW |

| 018 | 2026-05-20 tilemap perf fix | `penrose::nearest_vertex` replaced with a bucket-based spiral search (`build_vertex_buckets` + `nearest_vertex_bucketed` over the new `UniformBuckets<Vec2>` helper in `engine/placement/spatial.rs`). Bit-exact equivalent to the prior linear scan (AC-2 oracle: 800 random queries √ó 4 vertex-field configurations incl. 1500-vertex dense; AC-3 cross-bucket tie-break by lowest index). Per-stage continent measurement: `place_zones` (Penrose + fractalize) dropped to **0.110 s** at 256¬≤ ‚Äî well under the TMP_002 ¬ß7 <500 ms budget. Spec: [`docs/specs/2026-05-20-tilemap-perf-fractalize-penrose.md`](../specs/2026-05-20-tilemap-perf-fractalize-penrose.md). |
| 016 | 2026-05-20 tilemap perf fix | `fractalize::scatter_and_connect` replaced with a bucket-based "any within radius" rewrite using the same `UniformBuckets<TileCoord>` helper ‚Äî 3√ó3 ring neighbourhood query (sufficiency proven: `bucket_size = ceil(sqrt(coverage_sq))` ‚áí ring-2 min dist¬≤ ‚â• 49 > 36 surface / 25 > 16 sea). Bit-exact equivalent to the prior `nearest_cleared_dist_sq` linear scan (AC-1 oracle: 96¬≤ zone √ó 5 seeds √ó 2 spans). The naive impl is kept under `#[cfg(test)]` as the oracle. Spec: [`docs/specs/2026-05-20-tilemap-perf-fractalize-penrose.md`](../specs/2026-05-20-tilemap-perf-fractalize-penrose.md). |
| 022 | 2026-05-18 tilemap Phase E | Resolved by the Phase-E `RiverPlacer` design (spec D-Q5/D-Q6): the `anchor` representative point **suffices** ‚Äî no footprint extent needed. River sources are one lowest-flat-index `Mountain` obstacle anchor per mountain-bearing zone; sinks are the `Lake` obstacle anchors ‚à™ `Sea`-zone tiles. `search_path` routes to/from the anchor tile, which is added to the river search area. No additive `TilemapObjectPlacement` footprint field was required. |
| 019 | 2026-05-18 tilemap Phase D | Re-validated when ConnectionsPlacer landed. `seal_borders` (¬ß3.1/¬ß9) seals inter-zone borders but is not a complete wall ‚Äî passage corridors stay `Walkable` and gap-critical tiles stay `Open`. The per-zone `would_seal_a_gap` is sound regardless: every tile belongs to exactly one zone, and every passability-removing mutation (placer objects, monoliths, guards, ferries, border seals) targets an `Open` tile that is zone-interior ‚Äî so removing a zone-X tile can never split zone Y. Border-seal completeness is irrelevant to correctness; the original "border thickness" framing was the wrong lens. |
| 012 | 2026-05-16 tilemap fix task | Added `TestStreamOpenAICompat_MultipleToolCalls` ‚Äî two tool calls streamed with interleaved/non-monotonic `tool_calls[].index` (0,1,1,0); asserts `Index` is verbatim from the provider and per-index reassembly stays separate. |
| 011 | 2026-05-16 tilemap fix task | Dropped `nullable: true` from openapi `ChatStreamRequest.tool_choice` ‚Äî the field is now `description` + `oneOf` only (idiomatic OpenAPI 3.0; still optional via not-in-`required`). No runtime change; SDKs already model it optional. |
